findings.message: bulk_create_items() commits inside the loop, so a failure on any iteration leaves a partial batch persisted with no rollback path. This breaks atomicity of the bulk operation and can corrupt downstream invariants that assume all-or-nothing inserts (e.g. callers retrying with the full list will double-insert the already-committed prefix). It also defeats transactional bulk-insert performance.
findings.suggestion:
Add all items first and commit once at the end inside a single transaction, e.g.:

    items = [Item(name=name) for name in names]
    session.add_all(items)
    session.commit()
    for item in items:
        session.refresh(item)
    return items

Or wrap in `with session.begin():` so any failure rolls back the whole batch.

findings.message: No input validation on `names`: empty strings, None, or values exceeding the column's String(255) length will only fail at flush/commit time, and with per-iteration commits earlier rows are already persisted before the offending row raises. Validate (non-empty, length <= 255, de-duplicated if required) before any writes to preserve batch integrity.
findings.suggestion: Validate inputs up-front (e.g. assert all `n` is a non-empty str with len(n) <= 255) before adding to the session, and surface a single ValueError rather than a partial DB state.
